controls.name: Prompt Injection
controls.description: Manipulating LLMs via crafted inputs can lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, and compromised decision-making. Direct injections overwrite system prompts, while indirect ones manipulate inputs from external sources.

controls.name: Insecure Output Handling
controls.description: Failing to validate LLM outputs may lead to downstream security exploits, including code execution that compromises systems and exposes data. Common issues include XSS, CSRF, SSRF, privilege escalation, or remote code execution.

controls.name: Training Data Poisoning
controls.description: Tampered training data can impair LLM models leading to responses that may compromise security, accuracy, or ethical behavior. Sources include Common Crawl, WebText, OpenWebText, books, and other datasets.

controls.name: Model Denial of Service
controls.description: Attackers cause resource-heavy operations on LLMs, leading to service degradation or high costs. The vulnerability is magnified due to the resource-intensive nature of LLMs and unpredictability of user inputs.

controls.name: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
controls.description: LLM supply chains can be vulnerable impacting the integrity of training data, ML models, and deployment platforms. Issues include third-party packages, pre-trained models, crowdsourced data, and model hosting.

controls.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
controls.description: LLMs may inadvertently reveal confidential data in their responses, leading to unauthorized data access, privacy violations, and security breaches. Models can memorize and regurgitate training data.

controls.name: Insecure Plugin Design
controls.description: LLM plugins can have insecure inputs and insufficient access control, enabling malicious requests that could result in undesired behaviors or remote code execution. Plugins extend LLM capabilities but introduce risks.

controls.name: Excessive Agency
controls.description: LLM-based systems may undertake actions leading to unintended consequences when granted excessive functionality, permissions, or autonomy. The issue arises from excessive permissions or autonomy granted to LLM agents.

controls.name: Overreliance
controls.description: Systems or people overly depending on LLMs without oversight may face misinformation, miscommunication, legal issues, and security vulnerabilities due to incorrect or inappropriate content generated by LLMs.

controls.name: Model Theft
controls.description: Unauthorized access, copying, or exfiltration of proprietary LLM models. The impact includes economic loss, compromised competitive advantage, and potential access to sensitive information contained in the model.
